subject: [PATCH] mm: khugepaged: free xarray nodes left behind by failed collapse_file()

reviews.author: Jinjiang Tu
reviews.summary: Reviewer Jinjiang Tu noted that the patch only fixes one specific failure path (SCAN_TRUNCATED) and suggested adding more test cases to trigger other potential failure paths, making it easier to reproduce and debug issues.
reviews.raw_body:
I can reproduce it easily with following steps. [1]
1) create file /tmp/test_madvise_collapse and ftruncate to 4MB size, and
then mmap the file
2) memset for the first 2MB
3) madvise(MADV_COLLAPSE) for the second 2MB
4) unlink the file

This might be the only path easy to trigger; all other failure paths seem to be difficult to trigger.

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/all/20260121062243.1893129-1-tujinjiang@huawei.com/

reviews.author: Matthew Wilcox
reviews.summary: Reviewer Matthew Wilcox questioned the necessity of clearing the xarray nodes before reaching clear_inode(), asking if there's a problem that needs to be addressed.
reviews.raw_body:
Yes, but is there a problem?  That is, do we need to clear this up
before we get to clear_inode()?

reviews.author: Jinjiang Tu
reviews.summary: Reviewer Jinjiang Tu noted that freeing empty xarray nodes in clear_inode() is sufficient, as a user cannot exhaust memory by continuously creating xa_node structures. Additionally, they suggested not to free these nodes in the collapse_file() rollback path, as it may lead to concurrent calls of collapse_file(), which creates new xa_nodes for the same region.
reviews.raw_body:
A user cannot exhaust memory by continuously creating xa_node structures. So
it is enough to free emptyxa_nodes in clear_inode().

We shouldn't free empty xa_nodes in collapse_file() rollback path, because we
couldn't avoid a concurrent call of collapse_file(), which creates xa_nodes
for the same region and may use these nodes.

reviews.author: Rik Riel
reviews.summary: Rik Riel questioned the necessity of the patch, suggesting that leaving the xarray node hanging around until clear_inode() time might not cause any issues, and asked if large folios getting inserted into the xarray at a later time could lead to problems.
reviews.raw_body:
If there is no harm in leaving this xarray node hang around until
clear_inode() time, I agree we do not need this patch.

Are there no issues with large folios getting inserted into the
xarray at a later time, after zapping little ones, and leaving
the empty xarray node dangling?

If none of this causes issues, we can keep things simple.

reviews.author: Matthew Wilcox
reviews.summary: The reviewer noted that __filemap_add_folio() already handles empty xarray nodes by overwriting them with xas_store(), which in turn calls xas_free_nodes() to free the overwritten nodes, rendering the original patch unnecessary.

2026-06-17.patch_summary: This patch addresses a memory leak issue in the khugepaged code, specifically when collapse_file() fails and leaves behind empty xarray nodes. These nodes are not freed on error paths, leading to their leakage until the inode is evicted. The patch introduces a mechanism to prune these empty nodes by storing an XA_RETRY_ENTRY into each slot and immediately clearing it, allowing the node and its ancestors to be freed.
